Title: The Tell-Tale Heart
Writer: Edgar Allan Poe, Bill Scott, Fred Grable
Plot: A madman tells his tale of murder, and how a strange beating sound haunted him afterward.
Awards: Nominated for 1 Oscar. 1 win & 1 nomination total

Title: The Big Snit
Actors: Jay Brazeau, Ida Osler, Randy Woods
Plot: A couple have a fight over a scrabble game unaware that a full scale nuclear war has started.
Awards: Nominated for 1 Oscar. 4 wins & 3 nominations total

Title: Closed Mondays
Plot: A man visiting a museum sees the works of art come to life.

Title: Blitz Wolf
Actors: Leone Le Doux, Sara Berner, Pinto Colvig
Plot: Yet another variation on the Three Little Pigs theme, this time told as WW2 anti-German propaganda (the US had just entered the war), with the wolf as a thinly-disguised Hitler.

Title: The Sand Castle
Plot: On a sandy desert, a man made of sand constructs other sand creatures to help make a beautiful sand castle for themselves.

Title: Charade
Plot: A talented, but doomed, gentleman tries to participate in a properly British game of charades.

Title: The Cat Came Back
Plot: A pesky yellow cat becomes the bane of Mr. Johnson's life as it constantly outsmarts his increasingly desperate attempts to get rid of it.
Awards: Nominated for 1 Oscar. 2 wins & 1 nomination total

Title: The Bead Game
Plot: To a percussive soundtrack, a succession of more complex animals forms and is consumed by their successors (all formed from beads). Finally, We reach man, who develops ever more sophisticated forms of war.
Awards: Nominated for 1 Oscar. 1 win & 1 nomination total

Title: Cavallette
Plot: Men killing each other through the ages for various reasons.
Awards: Nominated for 1 Oscar. 1 win & 1 nomination total

Title: Pas de deux
Plot: Two ballet dancers perform a dance enhanced with surreal multi and after-image effect visuals.
Awards: Nominated for 1 Oscar. 4 wins & 2 nominations total

Title: Hunger
Plot: Cruel, and utterly effective, Peter Foldes' experimentation with computer animation employs a bold speechless narrative to draw attention to the grave effects of consumerism. Who needs ethics when everything is within arm's reach?
Awards: Nominated for 1 Oscar. 2 wins & 2 nominations total

Title: The Substitute
Plot: Simple story about the man who comes to the beach and uses inflatable objects for all of his needs.

Title: Three Little Pigs
Writer: Pinto Colvig, Albert Hunter, Boris V. Morkovin
Actors: Billy Bletcher, Pinto Colvig, Dorothy Compton
Plot: The Three Little Pigs each build a house of different material. The Big Bad Wolf comes along and blows away the straw and stick homes, but is unable to destroy the house of bricks.

Title: Gerald McBoing-Boing
Writer: Dr. Seuss, Bill Scott, Phil Eastman
Plot: The story of a little boy who would only talk in sound effects. With story by Dr. Seuss (and Bill Scott of Rocky and Bullwinkle fame) this cartoon won the Oscar for best short subject (animated) for 1950.

Title: Father and Daughter
Plot: A father says goodbye to his young daughter. Time passes and the daughter moves through life age by age, but within her there is always a deep longing for her father.

Title: Anna & Bella
Actors: Tonny Huurdeman, Annemieke Ring, Peter Ring
Plot: Two sisters look back on their life together.

Title: Tango
Plot: Subsequent characters appear in a poorly-decorated room, intertwining but never colliding, all possessed by never-ending rituals.

Title: Steamboat Willie
Plot: Mickey Mouse is a mischievous deckhand on a riverboat that is under the command of the tyrannical Captain Pete.

Title: What's Opera, Doc?
Plot: Elmer Fudd is again hunting rabbits - only this time it's an opera. Wagner's Siegfried with Elmer as the titular hero and Bugs as Brunnhilde. They sing, they dance, they eat the scenery.

Title: Creature Comforts
Plot: A humourous and thought provoking view of what animals in zoos might be thinking about their captivity and surroundings.

Title: The Street
Actors: Mort Ransen, Sarah Dwight, John Hood
Plot: This film deals with a Jewish family in Montreal, Canada as they care for a dying grandmother and the young boy who is impatient to get the room he was promised as soon as she kicks the bucket.
Awards: Nominated for 1 Oscar. 2 wins & 1 nomination total

Title: The Man Who Planted Trees
Plot: The story of a shepherd's single handed quest to re-forest a barren valley.

Title: Crac
Plot: The industrialization of Montreal (Canada), as seen from the point of view of a rocking-chair.

Title: Luxo Jr.
Plot: A larger lamp watches while a smaller, younger lamp plays exuberantly with a ball but doesn't pick up the knack of correct handling.
Awards: Nominated for 1 Oscar. 5 wins & 2 nominations total

Title: Duck Amuck
Plot: The short-tempered Daffy Duck must improvise madly as the backgrounds, his costumes, the soundtrack, even his physical form, shifts and changes at the whim of the animator.

Title: Dimensions of Dialogue
Plot: Three surreal depictions of failures of communication that occur on all levels of human society.

Title: A Fantasy
Plot: The first all-animated film in history, a series of scenes without much narrative structure, but morphing into each other.
